en.extract:
The violet crow (Corvus violaceus) is a species of the crow family, Corvidae, native to Seram, an island in Indonesia. It was long considered a subspecies of the slender-billed crow but has been shown to be divergent genetically. Violet crows have a dark black head and slightly blue black body and have a slightly shorter bill than most other crow species.


Habitat
Violet crows primarily inhabit forests and plantations, although they move to more open areas and farmland to feed.


References
http://www.hbw.com/species/violet-crow-corvus-violaceus

The '''violet crow''' ('''''Corvus violaceus''''') is a species of the crow family, [[Corvidae]], native to [[Seram]], an island in Indonesia. It was long considered a subspecies of the [[slender-billed crow]] but has been shown to be divergent genetically.<ref name="jonsson 2012">{{cite journal|author1=Jønsson, Knud A. |author2=Fabre, Pierre-Henri |author3=Irestedt, Martin |year=2012|title=Brains, tools, innovation and biogeography in crows and ravens|journal=BMC Evolutionary Biology|volume=12|doi=10.1186/1471-2148-12-72 |page=72 |pmid=22642364 |pmc=3480872 |doi-access=free |bibcode=2012BMCEE..12...72J }}</ref> Violet crows have a dark black head and slightly blue black body and have a slightly shorter bill than most other crow species.

==Habitat==
Violet crows primarily inhabit forests and plantations, although they move to more open areas and farmland to feed.<ref name="BOTW">{{cite book |last1=del Hoyo |first1=J. |last2=Collar |first2=N. |last3=Christie |first3=D.A. |editor1-last=del Hoyo |editor1-first=J. |editor2-last=Elliott |editor2-first=A. |editor3-last=Sargatal |editor3-first=J. |editor4-last=Christie |editor4-first=D.A. |editor5-last=de Juana |editor5-first=E. |title=Birds of the World |date=2020 |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology |location=Ithaca, NY, USA |url=https://doi.org/10.2173/bow.slbcro3.01 |access-date=30 August 2024 |chapter=Violet Crow (Corvus violaceus), version 1.0}}</ref>
